acceptance_criteria:
- Symphony accepts tracker.kind=beads configuration and validates it correctly
- a beads adapter can fetch candidate issues, refresh issue states by id, create comments, and update issue state through beads HTTP
- local end-to-end verification demonstrates Symphony dispatching at least one beads issue and recording progress/state changes back into beads
- execution doc captures the exact run commands and observed results
description: Replace the current Linear-only assumption in the local Symphony implementation with a beads-backed tracker mode that speaks to the new beads tracker API. The goal is a real unattended Symphony flow against beads items, not a mock or one-off script.
design: Modify the local Symphony Elixir prototype to support tracker.kind=beads plus endpoint/auth config, implement a beads adapter that fetches candidates, refreshes states, writes workpad comments, and updates tracker state through beads HTTP, then run the service against a local beads store with reproducible verification steps.
title: Run Symphony on a beads backend via a dedicated tracker adapter
